title: ‘Deutschlandlied’ dates to

subtitle: National anthem
fact: "Il Canto degli Italiani" is a patriotic song written by Goffredo Mameli and set to music by Michele Novaro in 1847, currently used as the national anthem of Italy.

title: ‘Dixie (song)’ dates to
subtitle: Folk song
fact: "Dixie", also known as "Dixie's Land", "I Wish I Was in Dixie", and other titles, is a song associated with the Southern United States first published in 1860.

fact: "Baa, Baa, Black Sheep" is an English nursery rhyme, the earliest printed version of which dates from around 1744.

fact: "The Twelve Days of Christmas" is an English Christmas carol and nursery rhyme.

title: ‘State Anthem of the Soviet Union’ dates to
subtitle: National anthem
fact: The State Anthem of the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics was the national anthem of the Soviet Union and the regional anthem of the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic from 1944 to 1991, replacing "The Internationale".

title: ‘Greensleeves’ dates to
subtitle: Folk song
fact: "Greensleeves" is a traditional English folk song.

subtitle: National anthem

fact: "Hey Diddle Diddle" is an English nursery rhyme.

fact: "Nearer, My God, to Thee" is a 19th-century Christian hymn by Sarah Flower Adams, which retells the story of Jacob's dream.

fact: The "March of the Volunteers" is the national anthem of the People's Republic of China.

fact: "Jack and Jill" is a traditional English nursery rhyme.

subtitle: National anthem
fact: "Poland Is Not Yet Lost", known in Polish as "Mazurek Dąbrowskiego" and formerly the "Song of the Polish Legions in Italy", is the national anthem of Poland.

fact: "Three Blind Mice" is an English nursery rhyme and musical round.

fact: "Rock-a-bye baby on the tree top" is a nursery rhyme and lullaby.

title: ‘Hymn to Liberty’ dates to

fact: The "Hymn to Liberty", also known as the "Hymn to Freedom", is a Greek poem written by Dionysios Solomos in 1823 and set to music by Nikolaos Mantzaros in 1828.

title: ‘Lift Every Voice and Sing’ dates to
subtitle: National anthem
fact: "Lift Every Voice and Sing" is a hymn with lyrics by James Weldon Johnson (1871–1938) written in the late 1800s and set to music by his brother, J.

subtitle: Children's song or nursery rhyme
fact: "There Was an Old Woman Who Lived in a Shoe" is a popular English language nursery rhyme, with a Roud Folk Song Index number of 19132.

title: ‘Stille Nacht Heilige Nacht’ dates to
subtitle: Hymn or carol
fact: "Silent Night" is a popular Christmas carol, composed in 1818 by Franz Xaver Gruber to lyrics by Joseph Mohr in Oberndorf bei Salzburg, Austria.

title: ‘Wilhelmus’ dates to
subtitle: National anthem
fact: "Wilhelmus van Nassouwe", known simply as "Wilhelmus", is the national anthem of both the Netherlands and its sovereign state, the Kingdom of the Netherlands.

title: ‘Shche ne vmerla Ukrainy i slava, i volia’ dates to
subtitle: National anthem
fact: The State Anthem of Ukraine, also known by its incipit "Shche ne vmerla Ukrainy i slava, i volia" and its original title "Shche ne vmerla Ukraina", is the national anthem of Ukraine.

subtitle: National anthem

title: ‘National Anthem of the Republic of China’ dates to
subtitle: National anthem
fact: The "National Anthem of the Republic of China", also known by its incipit "Three Principles of the People", is the national anthem of the Republic of China, commonly called Taiwan, as well as the party anthem of the Kuomintang.

title: ‘Old Mother Hubbard’ dates to
subtitle: Children's song or nursery rhyme
fact: "Old Mother Hubbard" is an English-language nursery rhyme, first given an extended printing in 1805, although the exact origin of the rhyme is disputed.

title: ‘Good King Wenceslas’ dates to
subtitle: Hymn or carol
fact: "Good King Wencesla(u)s" is a Christmas carol that tells a story of a tenth-century king of Bohemia who goes on a journey, braving harsh winter weather, to give alms to a poor peasant on the Feast of Stephen.

title: ‘Brazilian National Anthem’ dates to
subtitle: National anthem
fact: The "Brazilian National Anthem", also known as the National anthem of Brazil, was composed by Francisco Manuel da Silva in 1831 and had been given at least two sets of unofficial lyrics before a 1922 decree by president Epitácio Pessoa gave the anthem its definitive, official lyrics, by Joaquim Osório Duque-Estrada, after several changes were made to his proposal, written in 1909.

title: ‘The Queen of Hearts’ dates to
